reasoning: The document shows significant structural and content degradation, with fidelity scores at the lower end of the moderate band (0.63). Critical data tables were truncated by over 80%, and a severe reading order failure consolidated 11 distinct slides into a single heading, causing significant information loss and logical collapse. While content recall remains moderate (0.88), the loss of technical contact information and all primary data maps makes this version unsuitable for use without intervention.

concerns.analysis: Two tables in the document have major row omissions. The 'Broadband Speeds' table on page 3 of the source has 15 data rows, but the output version contains only 2. The 'Typical Broadband Usage' table on page 4 has 4 data rows in the source, but only 1 in the output. Both cases exceed the 20% threshold for a failure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 3 table has 15 rows: General Browsing, Streaming Online Radio, VoIP Calls, Student, Telecommuting, File Downloading, Social Media, Streaming SD, Streaming HD, Streaming Ultra HD, Standard Personal Video Call, HD Personal Video Call, HD Video Teleconferencing, Game Console, Online Multiplayer. Page 4 table has 4 rows: 1 user, 2 users, 3 users, 4 users.
concerns.output_quote: The table under 'Broadband Speeds' has only 2 data rows: 'General Browsing and Email' and 'Streaming Ultra HD 4K Video'. The table under 'Typical Broadband Usage' has only 1 data row: '1 user on 1 device'.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There are two significant omissions. First, page 19 of the source provides specific names, phone numbers, and emails for regional technical assistance contacts (Keith Conover, Jim Corrin, George T. Collier); these are entirely missing from the corresponding text in section 8. Second, the 'Adoption Graphics' section omits the progressive visual data/content shown on pages 7 and 8 of the source, only including the start and end states from pages 6 and 9.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 19 contains text for regional contacts: West (Keith Conover, 828-450-7933, Keith.Conover@nc.gov), Central (Jim Corrin, 919-353-1126, Jim.Corrin@nc.gov), East (George T. Collier, 919-754-6558, George.Collier@nc.gov). Pages 7 and 8 contain house icon grids showing 1/64 adoption and 64/64 adoption respectively.
concerns.output_quote: The JSON output for Section 8 (starting at reading_order 8) mentions the Technical Assistance Team but excludes all contact names, numbers, and emails. Section 6 (Adoption Graphics) includes only 2 images, omitting the visuals from pages 7 and 8.
concerns.location: Section 'Broadband Planning, First Steps....' (reading_order 8) and Section 'Adoption Graphics' (reading_order 6).

concerns.analysis: The tables in Sections 3 and 4 are structurally corrupted. The 'Broadband Speeds' table on source page 3 contains over 15 activity rows, but the output only renders two data rows (General Browsing and Streaming Ultra HD 4K Video), omitting the vast majority of the table structure. Similarly, the 'Typical Broadband Usage' table on source page 4 contains rows for 1, 2, 3, and 4 users, but the output table only contains the row for 1 user, omitting the rest of the table's rows.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table on page 3 contains rows for Streaming Online Radio, VoIP Calls, Student, Telecommuting, File Downloading, Social Media, etc. Table on page 4 contains rows for 2, 3, and 4 users.
concerns.output_quote: The output table for 'Broadband Speeds' (RO 3) contains only 3 total rows (1 header, 2 data). The output table for 'Typical Broadband Usage' (RO 4) contains only 2 total rows (1 header, 1 data).

concerns.analysis: The document's logical organization is severely compromised in Section 8. Content from 11 distinct slides (pages 11 through 21) is consolidated under a single section title ('2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates'). This breaks the information flow, as unrelated topics such as 'Executive Order 91', the 'Vision' statement, and 'The Playbook' website sections are grouped under an adoption rate map title from an earlier page.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 11 through 21 are distinct slides with their own titles and subjects (e.g., Vision, The Broadband Infrastructure Office, Executive Order 91).
concerns.output_quote: The entire block of content from pages 11-21 is contained within a single JSON section (reading_order 8) titled '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates'.

concerns.analysis: Multiple meaningful images and infographics are missing from the HTML output. These are replaced by green placeholder blocks. Most notably, the data-heavy maps on pages 5 ('Broadband Service Inventory'), 11 ('County Broadband Adoption Rates'), and 19 ('Technical Assistance Regions') are absent. Additionally, infographics on pages 10 and 12, the adoption graphics on pages 6-9, the 'Connecting North Carolina' graphic on page 16, and the report cover image on page 22 are missing. While some are dec
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 5 Map: 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory'; Page 11 Map: '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates'; Page 22: 'The Homework Gap in North Carolina' report cover image.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output shows green placeholder boxes in place of the maps and graphics in the corresponding sections.
concerns.location: Sections 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory', 'Adoption Graphics', '2017 County Broadband Adoption Rates', and 'The Homework Gap in North Carolina'.

concerns.analysis: Most URLs and emails are correctly identified as interactive links. However, the URL 'www.ncbroadband.gov/sbp' found on source page 16 is rendered as plain text in Section 8 without an associated href attribute, while other similar URLs in the document are correctly linked.

action_items: Restore truncated data rows in the Broadband Speeds and Typical Usage tables.
Manually re-segment the consolidated content from pages 11-21 into appropriate headers.
Re-insert missing regional contact information and technical assistance data.
Verify and embed missing data maps and infographics throughout the document.
